ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 / Winnti DLL side-load from non-standard staging directory
ucs.description: Hunts APT41's signature DLL side-loading tradecraft where a benign signed third-party binary (often Notepad++, VMware Tools, DTaskManager, FortiClient, or a renamed Microsoft helper) is dropped to C:\Users\Public, C:\ProgramData, C:\PerfLogs or C:\Windows\Temp and loads an adjacent attacker DLL to launch ShadowPad, Crosswalk, Winnti, MoonBounce or PlugX. The loader-from-staging-dir + adjacent-DLL pair is the high-fidelity APT41/BARIUM marker.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen dc(Processes.dest) as host_count values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_proc values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_hash) as proc_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\Intel\\*","*\\Recovery\\*") AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Microsoft\\*" AND Processes.process_path!="*\\WindowsApps\\*") AND NOT Processes.parent_process_name IN ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","TrustedInstaller.exe","WindowsUpdate.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\Intel\\*")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_name as side_loaded_dll, file_path as dll_path] | where host_count <= 3 | convert ctime(first_seen) ctime(last_seen) | table first_seen last_seen dest user process_name process_path proc_hash parent_proc side_loaded_dll dll_path host_count
ucs.kql:
let _StagingDirs = dynamic([@"\\users\\public\\", @"\\programdata\\", @"\\perflogs\\", @"\\windows\\temp\\", @"\\intel\\", @"\\recovery\\"]);
let _BenignLoaders = dynamic(["msiexec.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","wuauclt.exe","omadmclient.exe"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "ImageLoaded"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_BenignLoaders)
| extend _ParentDir = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| extend _DllDir    = tolower(FolderPath)
| where _ParentDir has_any (_StagingDirs)         // APT41 routinely stages loaders in Public/ProgramData/PerfLogs
| where _DllDir == _ParentDir                      // DLL adjacent to the side-loading exe
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256          // not the loader self-loading
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\")
| summarize FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp), HostCount=dcount(DeviceName), Hosts=make_set(DeviceName, 25), Cmds=make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 5) by ParentExe=InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentPath=InitiatingProcessFolderPath, ParentSha=InitiatingProcessSHA256, LoadedDll=FileName, DllSha=SHA256, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where HostCount <= 3                              // rare across estate = APT-shaped, not enterprise software
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _StagingDirs = dynamic([@"\\users\\public\\", @"\\programdata\\", @"\\perflogs\\", @"\\windows\\temp\\", @"\\intel\\", @"\\recovery\\"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
| where EventID == 7                              // Sysmon ImageLoad
| extend Image       = tolower(tostring(EventData.Image))
| extend ImageLoaded = tolower(tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded))
| extend Hashes      = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
| extend User        = tostring(EventData.User)
| where Image has_any (_StagingDirs)
| extend ImageDir = tostring(parse_path(Image).DirectoryPath)
| extend DllDir   = tostring(parse_path(ImageLoaded).DirectoryPath)
| where ImageDir == DllDir
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| where not(ImageLoaded startswith @"c:\windows\system32\")
| summarize FirstSeen=min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen=max(TimeGenerated), HostCount=dcount(Computer), Hosts=make_set(Computer, 25) by Image, ImageLoaded, Hashes, User
| where HostCount <= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 DLL Side-Load From Non-Standard Staging Directory
id: 8c2fa1b4-7e1a-4f18-9b0e-7b1cf41a2f01
status: experimental
description: Detects DLL side-loading patterns characteristic of APT41 / Winnti / BARIUM where a benign third-party signed binary in a writable staging directory loads an adjacent DLL.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/apt41-dual-espionage-and-cyber-crime-operation
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0096
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: image_load
detection:
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\Intel\'
      - '\Recovery\'
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '.dll'
  filter_system32:
    ImageLoaded|contains: '\Windows\System32\'
  filter_installers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
  condition: selection_path and selection_dll and not (filter_system32 or filter_installers)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate portable applications staged in user-writable directories
  - Some software updaters (whitelist by hash after triage)
level: high
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading is the single most reported APT41/Winnti tradecraft across Mandiant, FireEye, Kaspersky and Microsoft (BARIUM/Brass Typhoon) reporting — they prefer signed third-party loaders dropped to user-writable directories and have done so consistently across ShadowPad, Crosswalk, MoonBounce, Speculoos and Winnti 4.0 campaigns. The combination of (legit binary in staging dir) + (adjacent un

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 BITSAdmin transfer chained to schtasks persistence within 30 minutes
ucs.description: Hunts the APT41 install/persistence sequence where bitsadmin.exe pulls a payload over HTTP(S) (T1197 + T1105) and is followed within 30 minutes by schtasks.exe /create on the same host establishing scheduled-task persistence (T1053.005). This bitsadmin→schtasks chain has been documented in multiple APT41 intrusions (FBI/CISA AA22, Mandiant) and is rarely executed back-to-back by legitimate users.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as bits_time values(Processes.process) as bits_cmd values(Processes.user) as bits_user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=bitsadmin.exe AND (Processes.process="*/transfer*" OR Processes.process="*/addfile*") AND (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*") by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as schtask_time values(Processes.process) as schtask_cmd values(Processes.user) as schtask_user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=schtasks.exe AND Processes.process="*/create*" AND (Processes.process="* /tr *" OR Processes.process="* /TR *") by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | eval delay_sec = schtask_time - bits_time | where delay_sec >= 0 AND delay_sec <= 1800 | convert ctime(bits_time) ctime(schtask_time) | table bits_time schtask_time delay_sec dest bits_user schtask_user bits_cmd schtask_cmd
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin   = 30;
let BitsDownloads = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName =~ "bitsadmin.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/transfer","/addfile","/RAWRETURN")
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)https?://"
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project BitsTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, BitsAcct=AccountName, BitsCmd=ProcessCommandLine, BitsParent=InitiatingProcessFileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/create"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/tr\s"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner BitsDownloads on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (BitsTime .. BitsTime + WindowMin * 1m)
| project DeviceName, BitsAcct, SchTaskAcct=AccountName,
          BitsTime, SchTaskTime=Timestamp,
          DelayMin=datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, BitsTime),
          BitsCmd, SchTaskCmd=ProcessCommandLine,
          BitsParent, SchTaskParent=InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by BitsTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin   = 30;
let Bits = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\bitsadmin.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("/transfer","/addfile","/RAWRETURN")
    | where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)https?://"
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project BitsTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, BitsAcct=Account, BitsCmd=CommandLine, BitsParent=ParentProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\schtasks.exe"
| where CommandLine has "/create"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/tr\s"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner Bits on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (BitsTime .. BitsTime + WindowMin * 1m)
| project Computer, BitsAcct, SchTaskAcct=Account,
          BitsTime, SchTaskTime=TimeGenerated,
          DelayMin=datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, BitsTime),
          BitsCmd, SchTaskCmd=CommandLine, BitsParent, SchTaskParent=ParentProcessName
| order by BitsTime desc
ucs.rationale: FBI/CISA Joint Advisory AA22 and Mandiant's APT41 reporting (M-Trends 2020/2022) explicitly call out APT41's use of bitsadmin to retrieve second-stage tooling immediately followed by schtasks-based persistence — the same chain was observed during their U.S. state-government intrusions (2021) and against telco targets. Each command alone is noisy (admins legitimately use both), but bitsadmin /trans

_field_issues.message: `BitsTime` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`BitsAcct` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`BitsCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`BitsParent` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `BitsTime` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`BitsAcct` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`BitsCmd` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`BitsParent` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
